content: Hello! How can I help you today?

Quantify the impact of Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) processing parameters—specifically scan speed and laser power—on the porosity-induced fatigue life of Inconel 718. Compare these findings with Electron Beam Melting (EBM) benchmarks at elevated temperatures (650°C), citing primary data from technical manufacturing repositories.
1. Prioritize EBM Surface and Post-Processing Data
Your Auditor Agent can now calculate the "Thermal Half-Life" of the fatigue life improvement.

Shot peening (SP) serves as a critical surface enhancement for Inconel 718 components, particularly those operating at the 650°C threshold where the alloy maintains excellent creep-rupture properties [1, 2]. By inducing residual compressive stresses, SP parameters such as intensity and coverage are designed to delay fatigue crack initiation and hinder small crack propagation [3]. However, the effectiveness of these parameters is inextricably linked to the material's initial microstructural state
